title: alsine procumbens var. extensa
intro: Alsine procumbens var. extensa, also known as creeping saxifrage or procumbent saxifrage, is a plant species that belongs to the saxifrage family (Saxifragaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Alsine procumbens var. extensa include its ability to treat wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
